title: Dried blood spot biobanking protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5y5an","text":"This protocol presents methods for collection and biobanking of whole blood in the form of a dried blood spot (DBS) from anticoagulated whole blood drawn by standard phlebotomy. Leveraging Capitainer B50 cards, for a shelf stable consistent 50 ul volume DBS, this protocol may be inserted in the beginning of any workflow involving fresh anticoagulated whole blood, such as isolation of peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), in which only 75-150 ul may be available to spare. Resulting DBS are held at room temperature until elution for downstream applications enabling a more flexible workflow compared to standard whole blood biobanking methods. Accurate quantification of nucleic acid or other analytes through downstream applications may be achieved by correcting through a dilution factor, considering (i) phlebotomy tube anticoagulant volume and (ii) total volume whole blood drawn. This protocol supplements original work published by Capitainer AB, Uppsala, Sweden.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"oq3i4","text":"8.5 ml acid-citrate-dextrose (ACD) or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) phlebotomy tube (whole blood drawn)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wdn2u","text":"Capitainer® B50 dried blood spot device, Capitainer AB, Uppsala, Sweden","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lh6wb","text":"P200 wide bore tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ykclb","text":"P200 mechanical pipette","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"updd2","text":"Tissue culture biosafety cabinet","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"skolw","text":"50 ml conical","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtain whole blood drawn into ACD or EDTA phlebotomy tube.
Clean and sanitize tissue culture hood with 70% ethanol Contributed by users , wipe down with Kimwipe, and UV sterilize.
Allow biospecimen to come to room temperature ( 00:30:00 post-draw) and briefly spin to remove residual whole blood from the top of tube.
Spray phlebotomy tube and sealed Capitainer B50 sampling card pack Contributed by users into tissue culture hood with 70% ethanol Contributed by users .
Transfer total volume of whole blood into labeled 50 mL conical with 10 mL serological pipette tip to ensure whole blood is uniform prior to sampling.
Note total volume of whole blood.
Proceed with PBMC isolation or other primary phlebotomy tube application using the remaining whole blood volume.
Open the Capitainer B50 sampling card pack Contributed by users in the tissue culture hood (use within 00:30:00 of opening).
Label card with relevant project info prior to sampling on top card lid. Use gentle pressure to maintain integrity of fragile sampling channels.
Fold back the protective tab on the bottom of the card and secure in the slit in back to create declined sampling lanes. Do not touch sample inlets.
Slowly and smoothly pipette 75 µL of whole blood into one card lane until the sample indicator line has been filled. There should be 5 µL, (2-5 µl) whole blood remaining in tip.
Let card sit open at Room temperature for 00:30:00 . Upon successful sampling, only the control window should be red.
After the 00:30:00 wait period remove the adhesive seal on the bottom tab of the Capitainer B50 sampling card pack Contributed by users and collapse the folds to protect the sample inlets.
Label with any additional study info and tape closed for storage at Room temperature .
For standard B50 sampling methods, please see Capitainer AB, Uppsala, Sweden, Capitainer‱ B50 dried blood spot device: Instructions for Use, 2025. Available from: Manufacturer website.
Record all observations and storage locations in secure spreadsheet or LIMS databasesystem
creator: Taylor Johnson
